details.reasoning: The implementation comprehensively meets all rubric requirements: (1) INIObject.zig created with create, parse, and stringify public functions with correct JSGlobalObject/JSValue signatures; (2) Parse function correctly handles sections [section_name], key=value pairs, and both ; and # comments via parseINIIntoJSON; (3) BunObject.zig properly wired with lazy property callback getINIObject; (4) C++ BunObject.cpp hash table entry added with correct macro naming; (5) TypeScript definitions in bun.d.ts include full namespace with both functions and comprehensive JSDoc; (6) Parser is self-contained, converting INI to JSON internally without importing npm packages; (7) Code follows Zig conventions throughout: jsc.JSFunction.create, ZigString.static, @import patterns, proper error handling (bun.JSError!), and idiomatic Zig (ArenaAllocator, ArrayList, std.mem utilities). The implementation is well-structured with logical separation (parseINIIntoJSON, writeJsonString, stringifyObject, appendINIValue functions) and handles edge cases like Windows line endings, whitespace trimming, and special character escaping.
